headings.usage: Use for all page titles, section headers, and the main 'Quote' text on the generated pins.

body.usage: Use for all UI elements, inputs, descriptions, and metadata display.

primary.description: Pinterest Red - Use strictly for primary actions (Generate, Download) and brand alignment.

secondary.description: Slate 900 - Use for high-contrast UI elements, headings, and distinct buttons.

accent.description: Cool Gray 100 - Use for backgrounds, secondary buttons, and input fields.

background.description: Pure White for the canvas, Off-White for sidebar/panels.

success.description: Emerald Green - Use for 'Completed' status and success toasts.

grid_system.gallery: grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6

FileUpload.style: Border-dashed border-2 border-gray-300 rounded-xl p-12 text-center hover:border-primary/50 transition-colors bg-gray-50/50

structure: Middle: Quote Text (Centered, Z-index 10, relative, drop-shadow-md, font-bold)
Bottom: White Bar (Absolute bottom, w-full, h-16, flex center, Z-index 20)

MetadataPanel.style: Fixed right or Modal, Glassmorphism background

general: Use 'sonner' for all notifications (Upload success, Generation complete).
Use 'lucide-react' for icons (Upload, Download, Image, Sparkles).
Ensure the 'PinCard' component strictly follows the visual structure defined in the problem statement.
The 'Generate' button should trigger a mock process that iterates through the Excel rows and assigns one of the mock images randomly.
Include a 'dark mode' toggle in the sidebar, but optimize for 'Light Mode' as the default.
pin_generation_logic: For MOCK generation: Use the provided Unsplash URLs as the 'AI Generated Image'.
If a user uploads a template, ignore the mock images and use the template.
The 'Quote' text must be centered and have a subtle text-shadow to ensure readability over the background image.
The 'Bottom White Bar' must be absolutely positioned at the bottom, 15-20% height of the card, with the text 'Tap to learn more'.
excel_handling: Use 'xlsx' or 'papaparse' (if CSV) to parse the file.
Store the parsed data in a local React state (or Context) for the MVP.
export: Use 'html2canvas' or similar to capture the 'PinCard' DOM element as an image.
Use 'jszip' to bundle them for bulk download.

design: Avoid 'Inter' for Headings. Use 'Outfit' via Google Fonts.
Do not use generic gray borders. Use tinted borders (e.g., slate-200) or shadows.
Ensure all interactive elements have 'data-testid' attributes.
Use 'gap-6' or 'gap-8' for grids to allow breathing room.
accessibility: Ensure text contrast on the Pin Cards is high. Use text shadows if necessary on the background images.
All inputs must have associated labels.
